```yaml
name: Foo
NAME: Foo

on:
  SCHEDULE:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      foo:
        DESCRIPTION: foo
  repository_dispatch:
    TYPES: []
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      foo:
        DESCRIPTION: input
        type: string
    secrets:
      bar:
        DESCRIPTION: secret
    outputs:
      baz:
        DESCRIPTION: output
        value: ...
  push:
    BRANCHES: []

defaults:
  RUN:
  run:
    SHELL: bash

concurrency:
  GROUP: workflow group
  group: workflow group

jobs:
  test1:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    NAME: Test
    concurrency:
      GROUP: job group
      group: job group
    environment:
      NAME: env name
      name: env name
    defaults:
      RUN:
      run:
        SHELL: bash
    strategy:
      FAIL-FAST: true
    container:
      IMAGE: foo:latest
      image: foo:latest
      credentials:
        USERNAME: root
        username: root
        password: ${{ 'test' }}
    services:
      my_service:
        IMAGE: my_service:latest
        image: my_service:latest
        credentials:
          USERNAME: service-user
          username: service-user
          password: ${{ 'test' }}
    steps:
      - RUN: echo
        run: echo
```

## File: `testdata/err/context_availability.yaml`
```yaml
# run-name
# ERROR at env
run-name: ${{ github.actor }} ${{ env.GITHUB_ACTOR }}

# env
env:
  # OK
  FOO: ${{ secrets.FOO }}
  # ERROR
  BAR: ${{ env.BAR }}

# concurrency
concurrency:
  # ERROR at env
  group: ${{ github.ref }}-${{ env.FOO }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      # on.workflow_call.inputs.<inputs_id>.default
      foo:
        type: string
        # ERROR
        default: ${{ env.FOO }}
      bar:
        type: string
        # OK
        default: ${{ github.actor }}
      timeout:
        type: number
      bool:
        type: boolean
    outputs:
      # on.workflow_call.outputs.<output_id>.value
      aaa:
        # OK
        value: ${{ inputs.foo }}
      bbb:
        # ERROR
        value: ${{ env.FOO }}

jobs:
  test:
    # jobs.<job_id>.concurrency
    concurrency:
      # ERROR
      group: ${{ github.ref }}-${{ env.FOO }}
      cancel-in-progress: true
    container:
      # jobs.<job_id>.container
      # OK
      image: ${{ github.actor }}:14.16
      # jobs.<job_id>.container.credentials
      credentials:
        # ERROR
        username: ${{ runner.os }}
        # OK
        password: ${{ env.FOO }}
      # jobs.<job_id>.container.env.<env_id>
      env:
        # OK
        RUNNER_NAME: ${{ runner.name }}
        # OK
        ENV_FOO: ${{ env.FOO }}
      # jobs.<job_id>.container
      # ERROR
      options: ${{ runner.name }}
    # jobs.<job_id>.continue-on-error
    # ERROR at env
    continue-on-error: ${{ inputs.foo || env.FOO == '' }}
    defaults:
      # jobs.<job_id>.defaults.run
      run:
        # OK
        shell: ${{ env.SHELL }}
        # ERROR
        working-directory: ${{ runner.temp }}
    # jobs.<job_id>.env
    env:
      # ERROR
      OUTER: ${{ env.XXX }}
      # ERROR
      NAME: ${{ runner.name }}
      # OK
      BAR: ${{ inputs.bar }}
    # jobs.<job_id>.environment
    environment:
      # ERROR
      name: ${{ runner.name }}
      # jobs.<job_id>.environment.url
      # ERROR at secrets
      url: ${{ secrets.URL }}-${{ runner.os }}
    # jobs.<job_id>.if
    # ERROR at env
    if: ${{ github.ref == env.REF }}
    # jobs.<job_id>.name
    # ERROR at runner
    name: ${{ runner.name }} by ${{ github.actor }}
    # jobs.<job_id>.outputs.<output_id>
    outputs:
      # OK
      foo: ${{ runner.name }}-${{ env.FOO }}-${{ steps.hoge.conclusion }}
    # jobs.<job_id>.runs-on
    # ERROR at both runner and env
    runs-on: ${{ runner.OS }}-${{ env.SUFFIX }}
    # jobs.<job_id>.services
    services:
      nginx:
        # ERROR
        image: ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
        # jobs.<job_id>.services.<service_id>.credentials
        credentials:
          # ERROR because runner is not available
          username: ${{ runner.name }}
          # OK
          password: ${{ env.MY_PASSWORD }}
        # jobs.<job_id>.services.<service_id>.env.<env_id>
        env:
          # OK (#500)
          RUNNER: ${{ runner.name }}
    # jobs.<job_id>.strategy
    strategy:
      matrix:
        fooo:
          # ERROR
          - ${{ env.OS }}
          # OK
          - ${{ inputs.foo }}
          # OK
          - ${{ github.ref }}
        include:
          # ERROR
          - fooo: ${{ env.OS }}
            # OK
            inc: ${{ inputs.foo }}
        exclude:
          # ERROR
          - fooo: ${{ env.OS }}
      # ERROR
      fail-fast: ${{ env.SHOULD_FAIL }}
      # ERROR
      max-parallel: ${{ env.MAX_PAR }}
    # jobs.<job_id>.timeout-minutes
    # ERROR
    timeout-minutes: ${{ env.TIMEOUT }}
    steps:
      - id: hoge
        # jobs.<job_id>.steps.continue-on-error
        # OK
        continue-on-error: ${{ inputs.bool }}
        # jobs.<job_id>.steps.env
        env:
          # OK
          RUNNER: ${{ runner.name }}
          # OK
          FOO: ${{ env.FOO }}
        # jobs.<job_id>.steps.if
        # ERROR at secrets
        if: ${{ env.MY_PASSWORD == secrets.PASSWORD }}
        # jobs.<job_id>.steps.name
        # OK
        name: ${{ env.VERSION }} on ${{ runner.name }}
        # jobs.<job_id>.steps.run
        # OK
        run: echo 'Name is ${{ runner.name }}'
        # jobs.<job_id>.steps.timeout-minutes
        # OK
        timeout-minutes: ${{ inputs.timeout }}
        # jobs.<job_id>.steps.working-directory
        # OK
        working-directory: ${{ runner.temp }}
      - uses: owner/repo@ref
        # jobs.<job_id>.steps.with
        with:
          # OK
          message: '${{ job.container.id }} is running on ${{ runner.os }}'
  caller:
    uses: owner/repo/workflow.yml@main
    # jobs.<job_id>.with.<with_id>
    with:
      # ERROR
      some-input: ${{ env.HELLO }}
      # OK
      outer: ${{ inputs.foo }}
    # jobs.<job_id>.secrets.<secrets_id>
    # ERROR
    secrets:
      password: ${{ env.MY_PASSWORD }}
  concurrency-2:
    # jobs.<job_id>.concurrency
    # ERROR at env
    concurrency: ${{ github.ref }}-${{ env.FOO }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo hi
  environment-2:
    # jobs.<job_id>.environment
    # ERROR
    environment: ${{ runner.name }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo hi
  # jobs.<job_id>.runs-on (#280)
  test-runs-on:
    runs-on:
      # ERROR
      labels: ${{ runner.OS }}
      # ERROR
      group: ${{ runner.OS }}
    steps:
      - run: echo hello
  test-services:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # jobs.<job_id>.services
    # ERROR at env
    services: ${{ inputs.bool || env.FOO }}
    steps:
      - run: echo
        # ERROR at inputs because `id` allow no context
        id: ${{ inputs.foo }}
  # jobs.<job_id>.snapshot.if
  snapshot-err:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    snapshot:
      image-name: my-custom-image
      # ERROR
      if: ${{ env.FOO == 'foo' && runner.name == 'foo' && secrets.FOO == 'foo' }}
    outputs:
      foo: bar
    steps:
      - run: echo hi
  snapshot-ok:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        foo: [a, b]
      fail-fast: true
    needs: [snapshot-err]
    snapshot:
      image-name: my-custom-image
      if: ${{ matrix.foo == 'a' && github.ref == 'foo' && vars.foo == 'foo' && needs.snapshot-err.outputs.foo == 'foo' && strategy.fail-fast && inputs.foo == 'foo' }}
    steps:
      - run: echo hi
```

## File: `testdata/err/env_context_banned.yaml`
```yaml
# issue #158
on: push

env:
  # 'env:' at toplevel cannot refer 'env' context
  ERROR1: ${{ env.PATH }}

jobs:
  my_job:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      FOO: aaa
      # 'env:' at job level cannot refer 'env' context
      ERROR2: ${{ env.PATH }}
    steps:
      # 'env:' at step level can refer 'env' context
      - run: echo "$BAR"
        env:
          BAR: ${{ env.FOO }}
      # This still should be OK
      - uses: test/my-action@main
        env:
          OS: ${{ runner.os }}
          NAME: ${{ runner.name }}
  container-job:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container:
      image: node:14.16
      # 'env:' at 'container:' can refer 'env' context
      env:
        MYPATH: ${{ env.PATH }}
    steps:
      - run: echo hello
```

  label:
    # OK
    types: &created [created]
  check_run:
    # OK
    types: *created
  pull_request:
    # ERROR: 'created' is not a valid type in this event
    types: *created

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      # Valid anchor inside invalid anchor
      - &invalid_step
        # ERROR: 'run' and 'with' within the same step
        run: echo
        with: &valid_inputs
          neovim: true
      # ERROR
      - *invalid_step
      # ERROR
      - *invalid_step
      # OK
      - uses: rhysd/action-setup-vim@v1
        with: &valid_inputs
```

## File: `testdata/err/evaluated_template.yaml`
```yaml
on: push
jobs:
  test:
    strategy:
      # OK: Expanding object value
      matrix: ${{ fromJSON(github.event.labels) }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/cache@v4
        id: cache
        with:
          key: foo
          path: bar
      # OK
      - run: echo "${{ 'hello' }} ${{ true }} ${{ 42 }}"
      # OK: Any type
      - run: echo "${{ env.FOO }}"
      # OK: Expanding object value
      - run: echo "$FOO"
        env: ${{ matrix.env }}
      # ERROR: loose object, strict object, and array
      - run: echo "${{github.event}} ${{steps.cache.outputs}} ${{github.event.commits.*}}"
      # ERROR: null
      - run: echo "${{null}}"
```

on:
  merge_group:
    branches-ignore: bar
    branches: foo
  push:
    paths: path/to/foo
    paths-ignore: path/to/bar
    branches-ignore: bar
    branches: foo
    tags: v*.*.*
    tags-ignore: dev
  pull_request:
    paths-ignore: path/to/bar
    paths: path/to/foo
    branches: foo
    branches-ignore: bar
  pull_request_target:
    paths: path/to/foo
    paths-ignore: path/to/bar
    branches-ignore: bar
    branches: foo
  workflow_run:

    branches: foo
    branches-ignore: bar

jobs:

on: push

jobs:
  error:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        test:
          - ${{ github.retention_days }}
    steps:
      # ERROR: `matrix.test` is a number value
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.test.foo }}
  no-error:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      # Type of matrix.test falls back to any since number and bool are merged
      matrix:
        test:
          - ${{ 123 }}
          - ${{ true }}
    steps:
      # OK: matrix.test is any type
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.test.foo }}
```

```yaml
on:
  push:
    branches:
      - '**'
      - 'release/v12'
      - '[x]'
      - ' '
    tags:
      - '!*'
      - /v\d+/
      - v[0-
      - v[9-0]
    paths:
      -
      - '!'
      - 'foo\bar'
      - ' '
      - '  foo'
      - 'foo  '
      - |
        foo.txt
      - '.'
      - './foo/bar.txt'
  image_version:
    versions:
      - '!'
      - 'v[0-'

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    snapshot:
      image-name: test
      version: 'v[0-'
    steps:
      - run: echo hi
```

## File: `testdata/err/if_cond_constants.yaml`
```yaml
on: push

jobs:
  test-steps:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo Constant `true`
        if: true
      - run: echo Constant in expression
        if: ${{ false }}
      - run: echo Constant in quoted expression
        if: '${{ true }}'
      - run: echo Quoted constant
        if: 'false'
      - run: echo Trailing space
        if: 'true '
      - run: echo Newline
        if: |
          true
      - run: echo Space before constant
        if: ' true'
  test-job:
    if: false
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo 1
  test-snapshot:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    snapshot:
      image-name: test
      if: true
    steps:
      - run: echo 1
  test-complicated-expr:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo Always 42
        if: true && 42 || !null
      - run: echo Always true
        if: ${{ contains(format('{0} {1} {2}', 'foo', 'bar', 'piyo'), 'o b') }}
```

  edge-cases:

    steps:

        if: '${{ false }} '
      - run: echo This is true
        if: ' ${{ false }}'
      - run: echo This is true
        if: ${{ false }} && ${{ false }}
      - run: echo This is true

## File: `testdata/err/invalid_comparisons.yaml`
```yaml
on: push

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        o:
          - { }
        a:
          - [true]
        a2:
          - [{ }]
    steps:
      # comparing == to object with implicit conversion is not allowed
      - run: echo '${{ 'foo' == matrix.o }}'
      # comparing != to array with implicit conversion is not allowed
      - run: echo '${{ 42 != matrix.a }}'
      # comparing == array and incompatible array is not allowed
      - run: echo '${{ matrix.a == matrix.a2 }}'
      # comparing > to null is not allowed
      - run: echo '${{ 1 > null }}'
      # comparing > to bool is not allowed
      - run: echo '${{ false < true }}'
      # comparing >= to object is not allowed
      - run: echo '${{ 'foo' >= matrix.o }}'
      # comparing <= to array is not allowed
      - run: echo '${{ false <= matrix.a }}'
```

## File: `testdata/err/invalid_container_syntax.yaml`
```yaml
on: push

jobs:
  case1:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container: fooo
    steps:
      - run: echo container must be mapping
  case2:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container: {}
    steps:
      - run: echo container must not be empty object
  case3:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container:
    steps:
      - run: echo container must not be empty
  case4:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container:
      env:
        Foo: bar
    steps:
      - run: echo image is missing
  case5:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container:
      image: ''
      credentials:
    steps:
      - run: echo credentials and image must not be empty
  case6:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container:
      image: ubuntu:latest
      credentials:
        username: fooo
    steps:
      - run: echo password is missing
  case7:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container:
      image: ubuntu:latest
      credentials:
        password: ${{ '...' }}
    steps:
      - run: echo username is missing
  case8:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container:
      image: ubuntu:latest
      ports: 8080
      volumes: '/foo:/bar'
      credentials: 'user:pass'
      env: 'FOO=BAR'
    steps:
      - run: echo non-string values
  case9:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container:
      image: ubuntu:latest
      credentials:
        username: foo
        password: ${{ '...' }}
        foo1: bar
      foo2: bar
    steps:
      - run: echo unknown keys
  case10:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container: ''
    steps:
      - run: echo image must not be empty string
  case11:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container:
      image: ubuntu:latest
      credentials:
        username: fooo
        password: p@ssw0rd1234
    steps:
      - run: echo password must not be put directly
  case12:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    services:
      redis:
        env:
          CONFIG: foo
    steps:
      - run: echo image is missing in services
  case13:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    services:
      redis:
        image: 'ghcr.io/rhysd/my-redis'
        credentials:
          username: foo
        env:
          CONFIG: foo
    steps:
      - run: echo password is missing in services
  case14:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    services:
      redis:
        image: 'ghcr.io/rhysd/my-redis'
        credentials:
          username: foo
          password: p@ssw0rd1234
    steps:
      - run: echo password should not be put in workflow
```

    paths-ignore: /path/to/bar.txt

    tags-ignore: deploy/*

    branches: main
    branches-ignore: test
    tags: v*.*.*
    tags-ignore: deploy/*

## File: `testdata/err/invalid_json_in_fromjson.yaml`
```yaml
on: push

jobs:
  foo:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
          - string: ${{ fromJSON('"hello"') }}
          - null: ${{ fromJSON('null') }}
          - array: ${{ fromJSON('["foo", 1.2]') }}
          - object: ${{ fromJSON('{"bool":true,"array":[false]}') }}
          - invalid-1: ${{ fromJSON('"foo') }}
          - invalid-2: ${{ fromJSON('["foo"') }}
          - invalid-3: ${{ fromJSON('') }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      # OK
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.string }}
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.array[0] }}
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.object.bool }}
      - run: echo ${{ contains(matrix.array, matrix.string) }}
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.invalid-1 }}
      # ERROR
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.null }}
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.array }}
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.object }}
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.object.array }}
      - run: echo ${{ contains(matrix.object, matrix.string) }}
```

## File: `testdata/err/invalid_steps.yaml`
```yaml
on: push

jobs:
  test1:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      # 'run' is first
      - run: echo hello
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      # 'uses' is first
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        run: echo hello
      # 'shell' is specified so it must be 'run'
      - shell: bash
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      # Empty
      - null
  test2:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # Empty steps
    steps:
  test3:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - foo: aaa
        bar: bbb
      -
      - { }
      - shell: bash
        working-directory: /foo/bar
        with:
          foo: aaa
          bar: bbb
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with: foo
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        shell: bash
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        working-directory: bash
      - run: echo hello
        with:
          foo: foo
          bar: bar
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        foooo: unknown key
      - run: echo hello
        foooo: unknown key
```

## File: `testdata/err/issue151_child_of_child_job.yaml`
```yaml
name: Test

on: push

jobs:
  first:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      first: 'output from parent'
    steps:
      - run: echo 'first'

  second:
    needs: [first]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      second: 'output from second'
    steps:
      - run: echo 'second'

  third:
    needs: [second]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo '${{ toJSON(needs.second.outputs) }}'
      - run: echo '${{ toJSON(needs.first.outputs) }}'

  third-ok:
    needs: [first, second]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo '${{ toJSON(needs.second.outputs) }}'
      - run: echo '${{ toJSON(needs.first.outputs) }}'
```

on: push

# Issue #155: `env` should not be available in `jobs.<job_id>.if`
# https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/contexts#context-availability

jobs:
  test1:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: ${{ env.FOO == 'aaa' }}
    steps:
      - run: echo 'hello'
  test2:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: env.FOO == 'aaa'
    steps:
      - run: echo 'hello'
  test3:
    uses: org/repo/workflow.yml@v1
    if: ${{ env.FOO == 'aaa' }}
  test4:
    uses: org/repo/workflow.yml@v1
    if: env.FOO == 'aaa'
```

    steps:

    steps:

    steps:

    steps:
      - run: echo 'hello'

    steps:

    steps:
      - run: echo 'hello'

    runs-on:

    steps:
      - run: echo 'hello'

## File: `testdata/err/matrix_exclude_mismatch.yaml`
```yaml
on: push

jobs:
  object:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        os:
          - {'runner': 'ubuntu-latest'}
        include:
          - os: {'runner': 'ubuntu-latest'}
            gui: 'gnome'
        exclude:
          # ERROR: Unknown key
          - operating-system: {'runner': 'ubuntu-latest'}
          # ERROR: Type mismatch object v.s. string
          - os: 'ubuntu-latest'
          # ERROR: Type mismatch object v.s. array
          - os: ['ubuntu-latest']
          # ERROR: Type mismatch object v.s. array
          - os: {'runner': {'name': 'ubuntu-latest'}}
          # ERROR: Unknown value
          - os: {'runner': 'windows-latest'}
          # ERROR: Type mismatch in key added by `include`
          - os: {'runner': 'ubuntu-latest'}
            gui: ['gnome']
          # ERROR: Unknown value in key added by `include`
          - os: {'runner': 'ubuntu-latest'}
            gui: 'kde'
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os.runner }}
    steps:
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.gui }}
  array:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        os:
          - ['ubuntu', 'latest']
        include:
          - os: ['ubuntu', 'latest']
            gui: ['gnome', 'gtk']
        exclude:
          # ERROR: Value mismatch at first element
          - os: ['macos', 'latest']
          # ERROR: Value mismatch at second element
          - os: ['ubuntu', '22.04']
          # ERROR: Type mismatch at second element
          - os: ['ubuntu', {'version': '22.04'}]
          # ERROR: Type mismatch in key added by `include`
          - os: ['ubuntu', 'latest']
            gui: [{'name': 'gnome'}]
          # ERROR: Unknown value in key added by `include`
          - os: ['ubuntu', 'latest']
            gui: ['gnome', 'x11']
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os[0] }}-${{ matrix.os[1] }}
    steps:
      - run: echo '${{ matrix.gui[0] }} on ${{ matrix.gui[1] }}'
```

## File: `testdata/err/matrix_exclude_no_match.yaml`
```yaml
on: push

jobs:
  test:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        os:
          - name: Ubuntu
            matrix: ubuntu
          - name: Windows
            matrix: windows
        arch:
          - name: ARM
            matrix: arm
          - name: Intel
            matrix: intel
        exclude:
          - os:
              # This doesn't match
              matrix: macos
          - os:
              matrix: windows
            arch:
              # This doesn't match
              matrix: riscv
          - os:
              matrix: ubuntu
              # This doesn't exist
              foo: bar
          - os:
              matrix: windows
            arch:
              matrix: arm
              # This doesn't exist
              foo: bar
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo 'name is ${{ matrix.os.name }} and arch is ${{ matrix.arch.name }}'
```

## File: `testdata/err/merge_key_unsupported.yaml`
```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs: &inputs
      foo:
        type: string
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      <<: *inputs
      bar:
        type: string

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: env
        env: &default_env
          FOO: BAR
      - run: env
        env:
          <<: *default_env
          TEST: test
      - &default_step
        run: echo hello
        working-directory: /foo/bar
        shell: bash
      - <<: *default_step
        run: echo bye
```

## File: `testdata/err/outputs_of_action_skipping_inputs_check.yaml`
```yaml
on: push

jobs:
  logLatestRelease:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: octokit/request-action@v2.x
        id: get_latest_release
        with:
          route: GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/latest
          owner: octokit
          repo: request-action
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      # octkit/request-action skips inputs check, but outputs are still checked
      - run: "echo latest release: ${{ steps.get_latest_release.outputs.this_output_does_not_exist }}"
```

## File: `testdata/err/shellcheck_default_shell_detection.yaml`
```yaml
on: push

defaults:
  run:
    shell: pwsh

jobs:
  job-level:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        shell: bash
    steps:
      # ERROR: shellcheck checks this script
      - run: $Env:FOO = "FOO"
  step-level:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      # ERROR: shellcheck checks this script
      - run: $Env:FOO = "FOO"
        shell: bash
  step-level-2:
    # Even if pwsh is detected from runner or job's defaults
    runs-on: windows-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        shell: pwsh
    steps:
      # ERROR: shellcheck checks this script
      - run: $Env:FOO = "FOO"
        # Step-level `shell` is prioritized
        shell: bash
  step-level-1:
    # Even if pwsh is detected from runner
    runs-on: windows-latest
    # `defaults` is prioritized
    defaults:
      run:
        shell: bash
    steps:
      # ERROR: shellcheck checks this script
      - run: $Env:FOO = "FOO"
  custom-bash:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      # ERROR: shellcheck checks this script
      - run: $Env:FOO = "FOO"
        shell: 'bash -e {0}'
  custom-sh:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      # ERROR: shellcheck checks this script
      - run: $Env:FOO = "FOO"
        shell: 'sh -e {0}'
```

/test\.yaml:14:17: calling function "failure" is not allowed here\. "failure" is only available in .+ \[expression\]/

/test\.yaml:17:17: calling function "hashfiles" is not allowed here\. "hashfiles" is only available in .+ \[expression\]/
/test\.yaml:20:13: calling function "hashFiles" is not allowed here\. "hashFiles" is only available in .+ \[expression\]/

## File: `testdata/err/special_function_availability.yaml`
```yaml
# Check https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/contexts

on: push

jobs:
  test:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        # All special functions are banned by default
        errors:
          - ${{ always() }}
          - ${{ cancelled() }}
          - ${{ success() }}
          - ${{ failure() }}
          - ${{ hashFiles('...') }}
          # case insensitive
          - ${{ hashfiles('...') }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # jobs.<job_id>.if does not allow hashFiles
    if: ${{ hashFiles('...') }}
    steps:
      # jobs.<job_id>.steps.run only allows hashFiles
      - run: echo 'success? ${{ success() }}'
```

      - run: env

## File: `testdata/err/unexpected_keys.yaml`
```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    invalid_key:
    inputs:
      foo:
        invalid_key:
  repository_dispatch:
    invalid_key:
  push:
    invalid_key:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      foo:
        type: string
        invalid_key:
    secrets:
      foo:
        invalid_key:
    outputs:
      foo:
        value: ...
        invalid_key:
    invalid_key:

defaults:
  run:
    invalid_key:
  invalid_key:

concurrency:
  group: Foo
  invalid_key:

jobs:
  test:
    environment:
      name: Foo
      invalid_key:
    strategy:
      invalid_key:
    container:
      image: foo:latest
      credentials:
        username: root
        password: ${{ '...' }}
        invalid_key:
      invalid_key:
    runs-on:
      labels: ubuntu-latest
      invalid_key:
    steps:
      - run: echo hello
        invalid_key:
    invalid_key:

invalid_key:
```

## File: `testdata/err/unused_anchors.yaml`
```yaml
# ERROR: Unused
on: &anchor push

jobs:
  # ERROR: Unused
  test: &anchor
    # OK: Used
    runs-on: &anchor ubuntu-latest
    env:
      RUNS_ON: *anchor
    # ERROR: Unused
    steps: &anchor
      # OK: Used
      - &step
        run: env
        # OK: Used
        env: &default_env
          FOO1: BAR1
          FOO2: BAR2
      - run: env
        env: *default_env
      - run: env
        # ERROR: Unused
        env: &default_env
      - *step
      # ERROR: Unused
      - &step
        run: echo hello
```

  FOO: ...

    strategy:
      matrix:
        version_name: [v1, v2]
        VERSION_NAME: [V1, V2]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container: foo:latest
    services:
      redis:
        image: redis
      REDIS:
        image: redis2
    steps:
      - run: echo 'hello'
        env:
          FOO: ...
          foo: ...
      - uses: foo/bar@main
        with:
          foo: ...
          FOO: ...
  call:
    uses: owner/repo@main
    with:

      FOO_input: ...
    secrets:

## File: `testdata/err/workflow_call_job.yaml`
```yaml
on: push
jobs:
  # steps is only for normal job, uses is only for call job
  call1:
    uses: org/repo/workflow.yml@v1
    steps:
      - run: echo
  # with requires uses
  call2:
    with:
      foo: bar
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo
  # secrets requires uses
  call3:
    secrets:
      aaa: bbb
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo
  # uses is empty
  call4:
    uses:
  # relative path
  call5:
    uses: "./foo/bar/workflow.yml@main"
  # absolute path
  call6:
    uses: "/foo/bar/workflow.yml@main"
  # missing repo name
  call7:
    uses: "foo/workflow.yml@main"
  # missing ref
  call8:
    uses: "foo/bar/workflow.yml"
```

name: Test for workflow_dispatch input types
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      # no options for choice
      choice_input_no_options:
        type: choice
      choice_input_options_is_empty:
        type: choice
        options: []
      choice_input_empty_option:

        options: ['']

        options:

        default: bar

        default: foo

          - bbb
      string_with_options:

          - bbb
      environment_with_options:

        options:

          - bbb
      no_type_with_options:
        options:
          - aaa
          - bbb
      input_unknown_type:
        type: unknown
      number_invalid_default:
        type: number
        default: hello

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo hi

## File: `testdata/err/workflow_dispatch_type_check_inputs.yaml`
```yaml
name: Test for type check of workflow_dispatch inputs
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      string:
        type: string
      choice:
        type: choice
        options:
          - aaa
      boolean:
        type: boolean
      number:
        type: number
      environment:
        type: environment

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      # Unknown input name
      - run: echo "${{ inputs.select }}"
      - run: echo "${{ github.event.inputs.select }}"
      # Bool value is not available for object key
      - run: echo "${{ github[inputs.boolean] }}"
      # ... but github.event.inputs.* is available since its properties are all strings
      - run: echo "${{ github[github.event.inputs.boolean] }}"
      # String value cannot be index value of array
      - run: echo "${{ github.event.*[github.event.inputs.choice] }}"
      - run: echo "${{ github.event.*[github.event.inputs.string] }}"
      - run: echo "${{ github.event.*[github.event.inputs.environment] }}"
      - run: echo "${{ github.event.*[inputs.choice] }}"
      - run: echo "${{ github.event.*[inputs.string] }}"
      - run: echo "${{ github.event.*[inputs.environment] }}"
      # Number value is not available for object key
      - run: echo "${{ github[inputs.number] }}"
```

## File: `testdata/examples/builtin_func_special_checks.yaml`
```yaml
on: push

jobs:
  test:
    # ERROR: Key 'mac' does not exist in the object returned by the fromJSON()
    runs-on: ${{ fromJSON('{"win":"windows-latest","linux":"ubuntul-latest"}')['mac'] }}
    steps:
      # ERROR: {2} is missing in the first argument of format()
      - run: echo "${{ format('{0}{1}', 1, 2, 3) }}"
      # ERROR: Argument for {2} is missing in the arguments of format()
      - run: echo "${{ format('{0}{1}{2}', 1, 2) }}"
      - run: echo This is special branch!
        # ERROR: Broken JSON string. Special check for fromJSON()
        if: contains(fromJson('["main","release","dev"'), github.ref_name)
```

## File: `testdata/examples/comparison_strict_checks.yaml`
```yaml
on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      timeout:
        type: boolean

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo 'called!'
        # ERROR: Comparing string to object is always evaluated to false
        if: ${{ github.event == 'workflow_call' }}
      - run: echo 'timeout is too long'
        # ERROR: Comparing boolean value with `>` doesn't make sense
        if: ${{ inputs.timeout > 60 }}
```

test.yaml:7:24: undefined variable "unknown_context". available variables are "env", "github", "inputs", "job", "matrix", "needs", "runner", "secrets", "steps", "strategy", "vars" [expression]
/test\.yaml:9:24: property "events" is not defined in object type {.+} \[expression\]/
test.yaml:11:24: undefined function "startWith". available functions are "always", "cancelled", "case", "contains", "endswith", "failure", "format", "fromjson", "hashfiles", "join", "startswith", "success", "tojson" [expression]

## File: `testdata/examples/contexts_and_builtin_funcs.yaml`
```yaml
on: push
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      # Access undefined context
      - run: echo '${{ unknown_context }}'
      # Access undefined property of context
      - run: echo '${{ github.events }}'
      # Calling undefined function (start's'With is correct)
      - run: echo "${{ startWith('hello, world', 'lo,') }}"
      # Wrong number of arguments
      - run: echo "${{ startsWith('hello, world') }}"
      # Wrong type of parameter
      - run: echo "${{ startsWith('hello, world', github.event) }}"
      # Function overloads can be handled properly. contains() has string version and array version
      - run: echo "${{ contains('hello, world', 'lo,') }}"
      - run: echo "${{ contains(github.event.labels.*.name, 'enhancement') }}"
```

```
test.yaml:14:17: context "runner" is not allowed here. available contexts are "github", "inputs", "needs", "vars". see https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/contexts#context-availability for more details [expression]
test.yaml:18:17: context "env" is not allowed here. available contexts are "github", "inputs", "matrix", "needs", "secrets", "strategy", "vars". see https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/contexts#context-availability for more details [expression]

```

## File: `testdata/examples/contexts_special_functions_availability.yaml`
```yaml
on: push

env:
  NAME: rhysd

jobs:
  test:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        directory:
          # OK: 'github' context is available here
          - ${{ github.workflow }}
          # ERROR: 'runner' context is not available here
          - ${{ runner.temp }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      # ERROR: 'env' context is not available here
      NAME: ${{ env.NAME }}
    steps:
      - env:
          # OK: 'env' context is available here
          NAME: ${{ env.NAME }}
        # ERROR: 'success()' function is not available here
        run: echo 'Success? ${{ success() }}'
        # OK: 'success()' function is available here
        if: success()
```

```
/test\.yaml:19:24: property "platform" is not defined in object type {.+} \[expression\]/
/test\.yaml:21:24: property "dev" is not defined in object type {.+} \[expression\]/
test.yaml:34:24: property "os" is not defined in object type {} [expression]

## File: `testdata/examples/contextual_matrix_values.yaml`
```yaml
on: push
jobs:
  test:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest]
        node: [14, 15]
        package:
          - name: 'foo'
            optional: true
          - name: 'bar'
            optional: false
        include:
          - node: 15
            npm: 7.5.4
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    steps:
      # Access undefined matrix value
      - run: echo '${{ matrix.platform }}'
      # Matrix value is strongly typed. Below line causes an error since matrix.package is {name: string, optional: bool}
      - run: echo '${{ matrix.package.dev }}'
      # OK
      - run: |
          echo 'os: ${{ matrix.os }}'
          echo 'node version: ${{ matrix.node }}'
          echo 'package: ${{ matrix.package.name }} (optional=${{ matrix.package.optional }})'
      # Additional matrix values in 'include:' are supported
      - run: echo 'npm version is specified'
        if: ${{ contains(matrix.npm, '7.5') }}
  test2:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      # Matrix values in other job is not accessible
      - run: echo '${{ matrix.os }}'
```

```
test.yaml:16:24: property "prepare" is not defined in object type {} [expression]
test.yaml:26:24: property "foo" is not defined in object type {installed: string} [expression]
test.yaml:28:24: property "some_job" is not defined in object type {install: {outputs: {installed: string}; result: string}; prepare: {outputs: {prepared: string}; result: string}} [expression]
test.yaml:33:24: property "build" is not defined in object type {} [expression]

## File: `testdata/examples/contextual_needs_object.yaml`
```yaml
on: push
jobs:
  install:
    outputs:
      installed: '...'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo 'install something'
  prepare:
    outputs:
      prepared: '...'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo 'parepare something'
      # ERROR: Outputs in other job is not accessible
      - run: echo '${{ needs.prepare.outputs.prepared }}'
  build:
    needs: [install, prepare]
    outputs:
      built: '...'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      # OK: Accessing job results
      - run: echo 'build something with ${{ needs.install.outputs.installed }} and ${{ needs.prepare.outputs.prepared }}'
      # ERROR: Accessing undefined output causes an error
      - run: echo '${{ needs.install.outputs.foo }}'
      # ERROR: Accessing undefined job ID
      - run: echo '${{ needs.some_job }}'
  other:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      # ERROR: Cannot access outputs across jobs
      - run: echo '${{ needs.build.outputs.built }}'
```

## File: `testdata/examples/contextual_steps_outputs.out`
```
test.yaml:10:24: property "get_value" is not defined in object type {} [expression]
test.yaml:22:24: property "get_value" is not defined in object type {} [expression]
```

## File: `testdata/examples/contextual_steps_outputs.yaml`
```yaml
on: push
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      # Step outputs can be used in job outputs since this section is evaluated after all steps were run
      foo: '${{ steps.get_value.outputs.name }}'
    steps:
      # Access undefined step outputs
      - run: echo '${{ steps.get_value.outputs.name }}'
      # Outputs are set here
      - run: echo "foo=value" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        id: get_value
      # OK
      - run: echo '${{ steps.get_value.outputs.name }}'
      # OK
      - run: echo '${{ steps.get_value.conclusion }}'
  other:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      # Access undefined step outputs. Step objects are job-local
      - run: echo '${{ steps.get_value.outputs.name }}'
```

## File: `testdata/examples/cyclic_deps_needs.yaml`
```yaml
on: push
jobs:
  prepare:
    needs: [build]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo 'prepare'
  install:
    needs: [prepare]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo 'install'
  build:
    needs: [install]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo 'build'
```

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      # ERROR: 'set-output' workflow command was deprecated
      - run: echo '::set-output name=foo::bar'
      # OK: Use this instead

      # OK: 'debug' command is not deprecated
      - run: echo "::debug::Set the Octocat variable"

## File: `testdata/examples/expand_object.yaml`
```yaml
on: push
jobs:
  test:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        env_string:
          - 'FOO=BAR'
          - 'FOO=PIYO'
        env_object:
          - FOO: BAR
          - FOO: PIYO
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      # OK: Expanding object at 'env:' section
      - run: echo "$FOO"
        env: ${{ matrix.env_object }}
      # ERROR: String value cannot be expanded as object
      - run: echo "$FOO"
        env: ${{ matrix.env_string }}
```

## File: `testdata/examples/glob.yaml`
```yaml
on:
  push:
    branches:
      # ^ is not available for branch name. This kind of mistake is usually caused by misunderstanding
      # that regular expression is available here
      - '^foo-'
    tags:
      # Invalid syntax. + cannot follow special character *
      - 'v*+'
      # Invalid character range 9-1
      - 'v[9-1]'
    paths:
      # GitHub Action's path filter doesn't recognize '.'
      - ./foo/bar.txt

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo ...
```

```
test.yaml:9:13: constant expression "false" in condition. remove the if: section [if-cond]

test.yaml:23:13: if: condition "${{ github.event_name == 'push' }} " is always evaluated to true because extra characters are around ${{ }} [if-cond]

## File: `testdata/examples/if_cond_always_true.yaml`
```yaml
on: push

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo "DEBUG! ${{ github.event_name }}"
        # ERROR: It is always evaluated to false
        if: false
      - run: echo 'Commit is pushed'
        # OK
        if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' }}
      - run: echo 'Commit is pushed'
        # OK
        if: |
          github.event_name == 'push'
      - run: echo 'Commit is pushed'
        # ERROR: It is always evaluated to true
        if: |
          ${{ github.event_name == 'push' }}
      - run: echo 'Commit is pushed'
        # ERROR: It is always evaluated to true
        if: "${{ github.event_name == 'push' }} "
      - run: echo 'Commit is pushed to main'
        # OK
        if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref_name == 'main'
      - run: echo 'Commit is pushed to main'
        # ERROR: It is always evaluated to true
        if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' }} && ${{ github.ref_name == 'main' }}
```

## File: `testdata/examples/main.yaml`
```yaml
on:
  push:
    branch: main
    tags:
      - 'v\d+'
jobs:
  test:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        os: [macos-latest, linux-latest]
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    steps:
      - run: echo "Checking commit '${{ github.event.head_commit.message }}'"
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node_version: 18.x
      - uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.npm
          key: ${{ matrix.platform }}-node-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}
        if: ${{ github.repository.permissions.admin == true }}
      - run: npm install && npm test
```

## File: `testdata/examples/matrix_checks.yaml`
```yaml
on: push
jobs:
  test:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        node: [10, 12, 14, 14]
        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest]
        exclude:
          - node: 13
            os: ubuntu-latest
          - node: 10
            platform: ubuntu-latest
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    steps:
      - run: echo ...
```

## File: `testdata/examples/not_persistent_matrix_values.yaml`
```yaml
on: push
jobs:
  test:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        foo:
          - 'string value'
          - 42
          - {aaa: true, bbb: null}
        bar:
          - [42]
          - [true]
          - [{aaa: true, bbb: null}]
          - []
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      # matrix.foo is any type value
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.foo }}
      # matrix.bar is array<any> type value
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.bar[0] }}
      # ERROR: Array cannot be evaluated as string
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.bar }}
```

on: push

# ERROR: Available values for whole permissions are "write-all", "read-all" or "none"
permissions: write

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      # ERROR: "checks" is correct scope name
      check: write
      # ERROR: Available values are "read", "write" or "none"
      issues: readable
      # ERROR: "models" doesn't have "write" scope
      models: write
    steps:
      - run: echo hello
```

## File: `testdata/examples/popular_action_outputs.yaml`
```yaml
on: push

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      # ERROR: The step is not run yet at this point
      - run: echo ${{ steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit }}
      # actions/cache sets cache-hit output
      - uses: actions/cache@v4
        id: cache
        with:
          key: ${{ hashFiles('**/*.lock') }}
          path: ./packages
      # OK
      - run: echo ${{ steps.cache.outputs.cache-hit }}
      # ERROR: Typo at output name
      - run: echo ${{ steps.cache.outputs.cache_hit }}
```

## File: `testdata/examples/pyflakes_integration.yaml`
```yaml
on: push
jobs:
  linux:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      # Yay! No error
      - run: print('${{ runner.os }}')
        shell: python
      # ERROR: Undefined variable
      - run: print(hello)
        shell: python
  linux2:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        # Run script with Python by default
        shell: python
    steps:
      - run: |
          import sys
          for sys in ['system1', 'system2']:
            print(sys)
      - run: |
          from time import sleep
          print(100)
```

on: push
jobs:
  test:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        runner:
          # OK
          - macos-latest
          # ERROR: Unknown runner
          - linux-latest
          # OK: Preset labels for self-hosted runner
          - [self-hosted, linux, x64]
          # OK: Single preset label for self-hosted runner
          - arm64
          # ERROR: Unknown label "gpu". Custom label must be defined in actionlint.yaml config file
          - gpu
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    steps:
      - run: echo ...

  test2:
    # ERROR: Too old macOS worker
    runs-on: macos-10.13
    steps:
      - run: echo ...
```

## File: `testdata/examples/shell_name_validation.yaml`
```yaml
on: push
jobs:
  linux:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo 'hello'
        # ERROR: Unavailable shell
        shell: dash
      - run: echo 'hello'
        # ERROR: 'powershell' is only available on Windows
        shell: powershell
  mac:
    runs-on: macos-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        # ERROR: default config is also checked. fish is not supported
        shell: fish
    steps:
      - run: echo 'hello'
        # OK: Custom shell
        shell: 'perl {0}'
  windows:
    runs-on: windows-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo 'hello'
        # ERROR: 'sh' is only available on Windows
        shell: sh
      - run: echo 'hello'
        # OK: 'powershell' is only available on Windows
        shell: powershell
```

## File: `testdata/examples/shellcheck_integration.yaml`
```yaml
on: push
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo $FOO
  test-win:
    runs-on: windows-latest
    steps:
      # Shell on Windows is PowerShell by default.
      # shellcheck is not run in this case.
      - run: echo $FOO
      # This script is run with bash due to 'shell:' configuration
      - run: echo $FOO
        shell: bash
```

## File: `testdata/examples/untrusted_input.yaml`
```yaml
name: Test
on: pull_request

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Print pull request title
        # ERROR: Using the potentially untrusted input can cause script injection
        run: echo '${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}'
      - uses: actions/stale@v9
        with:
          repo-token: ${{ secrets.TOKEN }}
          # This is OK because action input is not evaluated by shell
          stale-pr-message: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }} was closed
      - uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          # ERROR: Using the potentially untrusted input can cause script injection
          script: console.log('${{ github.event.head_commit.author.name }}')
      - name: Get comments
        # ERROR: Accessing untrusted inputs via `.*` object filter; bodies of comment, review, and review_comment
        run: echo '${{ toJSON(github.event.*.body) }}'
      - name: Do something with checking skip
        # OK: This placeholder uses an untrusted input, but the input cannot be injected to the script
        run: if [ "${{ contains(github.event.pull_request.author.title, '[SKIP]') }}" = "true" ]; then echo "skip"; fi
```

  push:
    # ERROR: Incorrect filter. 'branches' is correct
    branch: foo
    # ERROR: Both 'paths' and 'paths-ignore' filters cannot be used for the same event
    paths: path/to/foo
    paths-ignore: path/to/foo
  issues:
    # ERROR: Incorrect type. 'opened' is correct
    types: created
  release:
    # ERROR: 'tags' filter is not available for 'release' event
    tags: v*.*.*
  # ERROR: Unknown event name
  pullreq:

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo ...

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      scheme:
        description: Scheme of URL
        # OK: Type is string
        default: https
        type: string
      host:
        default: example.com
        type: string
      port:
        description: Port of URL
        # ERROR: Type is number but default value is string
        default: ':1234'
        type: number
      query:
        description: Query of URL
        # ERROR: Type must be one of number, string, boolean
        type: object
      path:
        description: Path of URL
        required: true
        # ERROR: Default value is never used since this input is required
        default: ''
        type: string
jobs:
  do:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo "${{ inputs.scheme }}://${{ inputs.host }}:${{ inputs.port }}${{ inputs.path }}"
```

on: push
jobs:
  job1:
    uses: owner/repo/path/to/workflow.yml@v1
    # ERROR: 'runs-on' is not available on calling reusable workflow
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  job2:

  job3:
    # ERROR: 'with' is only available on calling reusable workflow
    with:

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

  job4:
    # ERROR: This workflow does not exist

## File: `testdata/examples/workflow_dispatch_input_types.yaml`
```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      # Unknown input type
      id:
        type: text
      # ERROR: No options for 'choice' input type
      kind:
        type: choice
      name:
        type: choice
        options:
          - Tama
          - Mike
        # ERROR: Default value is not in options
        default: Chobi
      message:
        type: string
      verbose:
        type: boolean
        # ERROR: Boolean value must be 'true' or 'false'
        default: yes
      age:
        type: number
        # ERROR: Number value must be parsed as a float number
        default: teen

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      # ERROR: Undefined input
      - run: echo "${{ inputs.massage }}"
      # ERROR: Bool value is not available for object key
      - run: echo "${{ env[inputs.verbose] }}"
      # ERROR: Number value is not available for object key
      - run: echo "${{ env[inputs.age] }}"
      # ERROR: `github.event.inputs` is also not defined
      - run: echo "${{ github.event.inputs.massage }}"
```

test.yaml:23:22: property "credentials" is not defined in object type {actions_runner_debug: string; actions_step_debug: string; credential: string; github_token: string} [expression]
```

## File: `testdata/examples/workflow_inputs_secrets_types.yaml`
```yaml
on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      url:
        description: 'your URL'
        type: string
      lucky_number:
        description: 'your lucky number'
        type: number
    secrets:
      credential:
        description: 'your credential'

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-24.04
    steps:
      - name: Send data
        # ERROR: uri is typo of url
        run: curl ${{ inputs.uri }} -d ${{ inputs.lucky_number }}
        env:
          # ERROR: credentials is typo of credential
          TOKEN: ${{ secrets.credentials }}
```

## File: `testdata/examples/yaml_anchor_usage.yaml`
```yaml
on: push

jobs:
  test:
    services:
      nginx:
        image: nginx:latest
        credentials: &credentials
          username: ${{ secrets.user }}
          password: ${{ secrets.password }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: ./download.sh
        # OK: Valid alias to &credentials
        env: *credentials
      - run: ./upload.sh
        # ERROR: Unused anchor 'credentials'
        env: &credentials
      - &recursive
        run: ./some_script.sh
        # ERROR: Recursively referencing the anchor
        env: *recursive
```

on: push

jobs:
  test:
    services:
      nginx:
        image: nginx:latest
        credentials: &credentials
          # ERROR: Credentials are embedded directly in workflow
          username: my-user-name
          password: P@ssw0rd
          # ERROR: Unexpected key 'email'
          email: me@example.com
      redis:
        image: redis:latest
        credentials: *credentials
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: ./do_something.sh

## File: `testdata/find_project/README.md`
```markdown
This directory is used for testing that actionlint can detect a repository root.

- `TestLintFindProjectFromPath` in `linter_test.go`
- `project_test.go`

`.git` directory is dynamically created when the test case is run because Git doesn't allow committing `.git` directory.
```

## File: `testdata/format/README.md`
```markdown
Files in this directory are used for testing `-format` option of actionlint. Test cases are in `linter_test.go`.

How to generate `test.sarif`:

```sh
cd /path/to/actionlint
make build

# Generate output formatting with jq
./actionlint -pyflakes= -shellcheck= -format "$(cat testdata/format/sarif_template.txt)" testdata/format/test.yaml | jq . > test.sarif

# Remove version because actionlint.version is empty string while running unit tests
sed -i 's/(devel)//' test.sarif

mv test.sarif testdata/format/
```

How to generate other files:

```sh
./actionlint -pyflakes= -shellcheck= -format '{{json .}}' testdata/format/test.yaml > testdata/format/test.json
./actionlint -pyflakes= -shellcheck= -format '{{range $err := .}}{{json $err}}{{end}}' testdata/format/test.yaml > testdata/format/test.jsonl
./actionlint -pyflakes= -shellcheck= -format '{{range $ := .}}### Error at line {{$.Line}}, col {{$.Column}} of `{{$.Filepath}}`\n\n{{$.Message}}\n\n```\n{{$.Snippet}}\n```\n\n{{end}}' testdata/format/test.yaml > testdata/format/test.md
```
```

## File: `testdata/format/sarif_template.txt`
```
{
    "$schema": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oasis-tcs/sarif-spec/master/Schemata/sarif-schema-2.1.0.json",
    "version": "2.1.0",
    "runs": [
        {
            "tool": {
                "driver": {
                    "name": "GitHub Actions lint",
                    "version": {{ getVersion | json }},
                    "informationUri": "https://github.com/rhysd/actionlint",
                    "rules": [
                        {{$first := true}}
                        {{range $ := allKinds }}
                            {{if $first}}{{$first = false}}{{else}},{{end}}
                            {
                                "id": {{json $.Name}},
                                "name": {{$.Name | toPascalCase | json}},
                                "defaultConfiguration": {
                                    "level": "error"
                                },
                                "properties": {
                                    "description": {{json $.Description}},
                                    "queryURI": "https://github.com/rhysd/actionlint/blob/main/docs/checks.md"
                                },
                                "fullDescription": {
                                    "text": {{json $.Description}}
                                },
                                "helpUri": "https://github.com/rhysd/actionlint/blob/main/docs/checks.md"
                            }
                        {{end}}
                    ]
                }
            },
            "results": [
                {{$first := true}}
                {{range $ := .}}
                    {{if $first}}{{$first = false}}{{else}},{{end}}
                    {
                        "ruleId": {{json $.Kind}},
                        "message": {
                            "text": {{json $.Message}}
                        },
                        "locations": [
                            {
                                "physicalLocation": {
                                    "artifactLocation": {
                                        "uri": {{json $.Filepath}},
                                        "uriBaseId": "%SRCROOT%"
                                    },
                                    "region": {
                                        "startLine": {{$.Line}},
                                        "startColumn": {{$.Column}},
                                        "endColumn": {{$.EndColumn}},
                                        "snippet": {
                                            "text": {{json $.Snippet}}
                                        }
                                    }
                                }
                            }
                        ]
                    }
                {{end}}
            ]
        }
    ]
}
```

## File: `testdata/format/test.yaml`
```yaml
on:
  push:
    branch: main
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.msg }}
        with:
          arg: foo
```

## File: `testdata/ok/allow_any_outputs.yaml`
```yaml
on: push

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: dorny/paths-filter@v3
        id: filter
        with:
          filters: ...
      - run: npm run lint:md
        if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.md == 'true' }}
```

## File: `testdata/ok/anchors.yaml`
```yaml
on:
  label:
    types: &types [created]
  check_run:
    types: *types
  push: &filters
    branches-ignore: test
    paths-ignore: path/to/foo
  pull_request: *filters
  pull_request_target: *filters

defaults:
  run:
    shell: &default_shell bash

env: &default_env
  FOO1: BAR1
  FOO2: BAR2

jobs:
  test1:
    runs-on: &runner ubuntu-latest
    env: &job_env
      FOO1: BAR3
      FOO2: BAR4
    steps:
      - &step
        run: echo ${{ env.R }}
        env:
          R: *runner
        if: &cond ${{ env.R == 'windows-latest' }}
      - *step
      - *step
      - run: echo ${{ env.FOO1 }}
        env: *default_env
        if: *cond
      - run: echo ${{ env.USER }}
        env: &cred_env
          USER: rhysd
          PASS: P@ssw0rd
        if: *cond
      - run: echo ${{ env.PASS }}
        env: *cred_env
        if: *cond
  test2: &job
    runs-on: &runner windows-latest
    env: *job_env
    defaults:
      run:
        shell: pwsh
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          ref: *runner
      - run: echo ${{ env.R }}
        env: *default_env
      - run: echo ${{ env.USER }}@${{ env.PASS }}
        env: *cred_env
      - run: echo "$SHELL"
        shell: *default_shell
      - *step
  test3: *job
  test4:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        runner: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest]
        include:
          - package: &package
              name: foo
              version: '0.1.2'
            node: &node
              version: '20'
              path: /path/to/node20
          - package: *package
            node:
              version: '24'
              path: /path/to/node24
          - package:
              name: bar
              version: '1.0.0'
            node: *node
          - package: *package
            node: *node
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    steps:
      - run: ./install_node.bash ${{ matrix.node.version }} ${{ matrix.node.path }}
      - run: npm install ${{ matrix.package.name }}@${{ matrix.package.version }}
  test5:
    container:
      image: 'example.com/owner/image'
      credentials: &cred
        username: ${{ secrets.user }}
        password: ${{ secrets.password }}
    services:
      redis:
        image: redis:latest
        credentials: *cred
      nginx:
        image: nginx:latest
        credentials: *cred
    runs-on: ubuntu-slim
    steps:
      - run: ./login.bash ${{ env.username }}@${{ env.password }}
        env: *cred
  test6:
    env: &expr ${{ fromJSON('{}') }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-slim
    steps:
      - run: echo hello
        env: *expr
```

## File: `testdata/ok/container_syntax.yaml`
```yaml
on: push

jobs:
  test1:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container: 'ubuntu:latest'
    steps:
      - run: echo hello
  test2:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container: null
    steps:
      - run: echo hello
  test3:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container:
      image: 'ghcr.io/rhysd/test'
      env:
        FOO: foo
      ports:
        - 8080
        - 8081
      volumes:
        - '/foo:/bar'
      credentials:
        username: rhysd
        password: '${{ secrets.PASS }}'
    steps:
      - run: echo hello
  test4:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    services:
      redis:
        image: 'ghcr.io/rhysd/my-redis'
        env:
          FOO: foo
        ports:
          - 8080
          - 8081
        volumes:
          - '/foo:/bar'
        credentials:
          username: rhysd
          password: '${{ secrets.PASS }}'
    steps:
      - run: echo hello
```

on: push

jobs:

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:

          entrypoint: /bin/bash
          args: '-c "echo $INPUT_FOOOO"'
          foooo: Hello, world!
      - uses: 'docker://ubuntu:latest'
        with:
          args: '-c "echo $INPUT_FOOOO"'
          foooo: Hello, world!
      - uses: 'docker://ubuntu:latest'
        with:
          entrypoint: /bin/bash
          foooo: Hello, world!
      - uses: 'docker://ubuntu:latest'
        with:
          foooo: Hello, world!
      - uses: 'docker://ubuntu:latest'
        with:
          entrypoint: /bin/bash
          args: '-c "echo $INPUT_FOOOO"'
      - uses: 'docker://ubuntu:latest'
        with:
          args: '-c "echo $INPUT_FOOOO"'

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      services:
        required: true
        type: string
        description: contents of services.json

jobs:
  build-lint-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        service: ${{ fromJSON(inputs.services) }}
        exclude:
          # `service` is loaded from JSON. Check should be skipped
          - service:
              npm_project: foo
    steps:
      - run: echo 1

## File: `testdata/ok/issue-151-child-of-child-job.yaml`
```yaml
name: Test

on: push

jobs:
  first:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      first: 'output from parent'
    steps:
      - run: echo 'first'

  second:
    needs: [first]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      second: 'output from second'
    steps:
      - run: echo 'second'

  third:
    needs: [first, second]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo '${{ toJSON(needs.second.outputs) }}'
      - run: echo '${{ toJSON(needs.first.outputs) }}'
```

    inputs:

        type: string

      input2:
        type: boolean

## File: `testdata/ok/issue-249_matrix_exclude_filter_object_subset.yaml`
```yaml
name: 'Regression test case for issue #249'
on: push

jobs:
  test:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        os:
          - name: Ubuntu
            matrix: ubuntu
          - name: Windows
            matrix: windows
        arch:
          - name: ARM
            matrix: arm
          - name: Intel
            matrix: intel
        exclude:
          # This should filter out { os: { name: Windows, matrix: windows }, arch: {name: ARM, matrix: arm } }
          - os:
              matrix: windows
            arch:
              matrix: arm
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo 'name is ${{ matrix.os.name }} and arch is ${{ matrix.arch.name }}'
```

## File: `testdata/ok/issue-261_matrix_row_item_constructed_with_expr.yaml`
```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      custom-build-type:
        type: string

jobs:
  build_matrix:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        build-type:
          - debug
          - ${{ fromJson(inputs.custom-build-type) }}
        exclude:
          # 'release' is not listed in the matrix row, but it should not be reported as array
          # since one of the element of the row array is dynamically constructed with ${{ }} (#261)
          - build-type: release
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.build-type }}
```

## File: `testdata/ok/issue-263-inputs-shared-by-multiple-events.yaml`
```yaml
# Issue #263
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      input-for-dispatch:
        type: string
      same-type-input:
        type: string
      different-type-input:
        type: boolean
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      input-for-call:
        type: string
      same-type-input:
        type: string
      different-type-input:
        type: number

jobs:
  some-job:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo '${{ inputs.input-for-dispatch }}'
      - run: echo '${{ inputs.input-for-call }}'
      - run: echo '${{ inputs.same-type-input }}'
      - run: echo '${{ inputs.different-type-input }}'
```

on: push

jobs:

  test2:
    runs-on:
      labels: x64

    steps:

## File: `testdata/ok/issue-312_expression_in_env_var.yaml`
```yaml
on: push

env:
  ${{ github.event_name }}: ''
  foo-${{ github.event_name }}: ''
  ${{ github.event_name }}-bar: ''
  ${{ github.event_name }}-${{ github.event_path }}: ''

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container:
      image: node:14.16
      env:
        ${{ github.event_name }}: ''
        foo-${{ github.event_name }}: ''
        ${{ github.event_name }}-bar: ''
        ${{ github.event_name }}-${{ github.event_path }}: ''
    env:
      ${{ github.event_name }}: ''
      foo-${{ github.event_name }}: ''
      ${{ github.event_name }}-bar: ''
      ${{ github.event_name }}-${{ github.event_path }}: ''
    steps:
      - run: echo "$push"
        env:
          ${{ github.event_name }}: ''
          foo-${{ github.event_name }}: ''
          ${{ github.event_name }}-bar: ''
          ${{ github.event_name }}-${{ github.event_path }}: ''
```

## File: `testdata/ok/issue-384_narrow-logical-op.yaml`
```yaml
on: push

jobs:
  and_op_narrowing:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # `timeout-minutes` only allow number type value
    timeout-minutes: ${{ '...' && 60 || 20 }}
    steps:
      - run: echo hello
        # `timeout-minutes` only allow number type value
        timeout-minutes: ${{ '...' && 60 || 20 }}
  or_op_narrowing:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # `timeout-minutes` only allow number type value
    timeout-minutes: ${{ ('...' || 60) && 20 }}
    steps:
      - run: echo hello
        # `timeout-minutes` only allow number type value
        timeout-minutes: ${{ ('...' || 60) && 20 }}
```

  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:

          name: my-artifact
        id: dl
      # This output is missing in action.yml of actions/download-artifact@v3-node20 (#442).

## File: `testdata/ok/issue-66.yaml`
```yaml
on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
jobs:
  job1:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo 'job1'
  job2:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo 'job2'
  job3:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo 'job3'
  notify:
    if: always()
    needs: [job1, job2, job3]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: ScribeMD/slack-templates@0
        with:
          bot-token: ${{ secrets.SLACK_TEMPLATES_BOT_TOKEN }}
          channel-id: ${{ secrets.SLACK_TEMPLATES_CHANNEL_ID }}
          template: result
          results: "${{ join(needs.*.result, ' ') }}"
```

## File: `testdata/ok/issue-87.yaml`
```yaml
name: Lint Code Base

on:
  push:
  workflow_call:

jobs:
  build:
    name: Lint Code Base
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout Code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Lint Code Base
        uses: github/super-linter@v4
        env:
          VALIDATE_ALL_CODEBASE: false
          DEFAULT_BRANCH: master
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
```

## File: `testdata/ok/matrix_exclude_match_to_expr.yaml`
```yaml
on: push

jobs:
  nested-0:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        foo:
          - ${{ fromJSON('{"bar":"x"}') }}
        exclude:
          - foo: bar
    steps:
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.foo.bar }}
  nested-object-1:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        foo:
          - bar: ${{ fromJSON('"x"') }}
        exclude:
          - foo:
              bar: ...
    steps:
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.foo.bar }}
  nested-array-1:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        foo:
          - ["${{ fromJSON('\"x\"') }}"]
        exclude:
          - foo: ['...']
    steps:
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.foo[0] }}
```

## File: `testdata/ok/matrix_expression_in_exclude.yaml`
```yaml
on: push

jobs:
  not-nested:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        foo:
          - aaa
        exclude:
          - foo: ${{ fromJSON('"x"') }}
    steps:
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.foo }}
  nested-obj-1:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        foo:
          - bar: aaa
        exclude:
          - foo: ${{ fromJSON('"x"') }}
          - foo:
              bar: ${{ fromJSON('"x"') }}
    steps:
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.foo.bar }}
  nested-obj-2:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        foo:
          - bar:
              piyo: aaa
        exclude:
          - foo: ${{ fromJSON('"x"') }}
          - foo:
              bar: ${{ fromJSON('"x"') }}
          - foo:
              bar:
                piyo: ${{ fromJSON('"x"') }}
    steps:
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.foo.bar.piyo }}
  nested-array-1:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        foo:
          - [aaa]
        exclude:
          - foo: ${{ fromJSON('"x"') }}
          - foo:
            - ${{ fromJSON('"x"') }}
    steps:
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.foo[0] }}
  nested-array-2:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        foo:
          - [[aaa]]
        exclude:
          - foo: ${{ fromJSON('"x"') }}
          - foo:
            - ${{ fromJSON('"x"') }}
          - foo:
            - ["${{ fromJSON('\"x\"') }}"]
    steps:
      - run: echo ${{ matrix.foo[0][0] }}
```

    inputs:
      username:
        required: true
        type: string
    secrets:
      token:
        required: true
    outputs:
      firstword:
        value: ${{ jobs.example_job.outputs.output1 }}

jobs:
  example_job:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      output1: ${{ steps.step1.outputs.firstword }}
    steps:
      - id: step1
        run: echo "firstword=hello, ${{ inputs.username }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        env:
          TOKEN: ${{ secrets.token }}
```

on: push
jobs:
  test:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        ver: [0, 1]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: echo 'my repo'
        # This was not ok until https://github.com/github/docs/pull/8786
        if: contains(github.repository, 'rhysd')
      - run: echo 'my repo'
        # This was not ok until https://github.com/github/docs/pull/8786
        if: github.repository != 'rhysd/foo' && matrix.ver > 0 || !(github.token == '')
```

## File: `testdata/ok/pyflakes_python_shell.yaml`
```yaml
on: push
defaults:
  run:
    shell: python
jobs:
  step-level:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: print('hello')
        shell: python
      - run: print('hello')
        shell: 'python {0}'
  job-level:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        shell: python
    steps:
      - run: print('hello')
  workflow-level:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: print('hello')
```

  schedule:

    # With another timezone

    # Without timezone (still valid)

## File: `testdata/ok/shellcheck_step_shell.yaml`
```yaml
on: push
jobs:
  test:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest]
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    steps:
      # This causes SC1001 if `shell:` is not set explicitly
      - run: cat xxx\yyy.txt
        if: ${{ matrix.os == 'windows-latest' }}
        shell: pwsh
      # This causes SC1001 if `shell:` is not set explicitly
      - run: cat xxx\yyy.txt
        if: ${{ matrix.os == 'windows-latest' }}
        shell: 'pwsh {0}'
```

name: Log latest release

  push:
    branches:
      - master

jobs:

    steps:
      - uses: octokit/request-action@v2.x
        id: get_latest_release
        with:
          route: GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/latest

## File: `testdata/ok/special_function_availability.yaml`
```yaml
# Check using special functions at appropriate places doesn't cause any error
# https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/contexts

on: push

jobs:
  # jobs.<job_id>.if
  if-always:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: always()
    steps:
      - run: echo ...
  if-cancelled:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: cancelled()
    steps:
      - run: echo ...
  if-success:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: success()
    steps:
      - run: echo ...
  if-failure:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: failure()
    steps:
      - run: echo ...
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      -
        # jobs.<job_id>.steps.run
        run: echo "${{ hashFiles('...') }}"
        # jobs.<job_id>.steps.name
        name: ${{ hashFiles('...') }}
        # jobs.<job_id>.steps.continue-on-error
        continue-on-error: ${{ hashFiles('...') != '' }}
        # jobs.<job_id>.steps.env
        env:
          FOO: ${{ hashFiles('...') }}
        # jobs.<job_id>.steps.timeout-minutes
        timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJSON(hashFiles('...')) }}
        # jobs.<job_id>.steps.working-directory
        working-directory: ${{ hashFiles('...') }}
      - uses: owner/repo@ref
        # jobs.<job_id>.steps.with
        with:
          foo: ${{ hashFiles('...') }}
      # jobs.<job_id>.steps.if
      - run: echo ...
        if: always()
      - run: echo ...
        if: cancelled()
      - run: echo ...
        if: success()
      - run: echo ...
        if: failure()
      - run: echo ...
        if: hashFiles('...') == ''
```

## File: `testdata/ok/untrusted_inputs.yaml`
```yaml
name: Test
on: push

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      # Direct access to untrusted inputs
      - run: |
          echo "ISSUE: $ISSUE, COMMITS: $COMMIT, REF: $REF"
        env:
          ISSUE: ${{ github.event.issue.body }}
          COMMITS: ${{ toJSON(github.event.commits.*.message) }}
          REF: ${{ github.head_ref }}
      # Indirect access to untrusted inputs via object filter
      - run: |
          echo "BODIES: $BODIES, EMAILS: $EMAILS"
        env:
          BODIES: ${{ toJSON(github.event.*.body) }}
          EMAILS: ${{ toJSON(github.event.*.*.email) }}
```

name: Call a reusable workflow and use its outputs

on:
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  job1:
    uses: octo-org/example-repo/.github/workflows/called-workflow.yml@v1

  job2:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: job1
    steps:
      - run: echo ${{ needs.job1.outputs.firstword }} ${{ needs.job1.outputs.secondword }}
```

## File: `testdata/ok/valid_comparisons.yaml`
```yaml
on: push

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        o:
          - { }
        a:
          - [true]
    steps:
      # string is converted to number implicitly
      - run: echo '${{ '42' == 42 }}'
      # bool is converted to number implicitly
      - run: echo '${{ true == 1 }}'
      # null is converted to number implicitly
      - run: echo '${{ null == 0 }}'
      # string and bool implicit conversions are allowed though it is problematic
      - run: echo '${{ '1' == true }}'
      # string and null implicit conversions are allowed though it is problematic
      - run: echo '${{ '0' == null }}'
      # comparing null to any value is allowed
      - run: echo '${{ null == matrix.o }}'
      # comparing object to object is allowed
      - run: echo '${{ matrix.o == matrix.o }}'
      # comparing object to object is allowed
      - run: echo '${{ matrix.a == matrix.a }}'
      # string is converted to number implicitly on <
      - run: echo '${{ '41' < 42 }}'
```

## File: `testdata/ok/variables.yaml`
```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
env:
  # Setting an environment variable with the value of a configuration variable
  env_var: ${{ vars.ENV_CONTEXT_VAR }}

jobs:
  display-variables:
    name: ${{ vars.JOB_NAME }}
    # You can use configuration variables with the `vars` context for dynamic jobs
    if: ${{ vars.USE_VARIABLES == 'true' }}
    runs-on: ${{ vars.RUNNER }}
    environment: ${{ vars.ENVIRONMENT_STAGE }}
    steps:
      - name: Use variables
        run: |
          echo "repository variable : ${{ vars.REPOSITORY_VAR }}"
          echo "organization variable : ${{ vars.ORGANIZATION_VAR }}"
          echo "overridden variable : ${{ vars.OVERRIDE_VAR }}"
          echo "variable from shell environment : $env_var"

      - name: ${{ vars.HELLO_WORLD_STEP }}
        if: ${{ vars.HELLO_WORLD_ENABLED == 'true' }}
        uses: actions/hello-world-javascript-action@main
        with:
          who-to-greet: ${{ vars.GREET_NAME }}
```

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      input0:
        description: 'hello'
        type: string
      input1:
        description: 'with default and required'
        default: 'hello'
        required: false
        type: string
      input2:
        description: 'with default'
        default: 23
        type: number
      input3:
        description: 'with required'
        required: true
        type: boolean
    secrets:
      secret0:
        description: '🙊'
      secret1:
        description: 'with required'
        required: true

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-24.04
    steps:
      - run: |
          echo ${{ inputs.input0 }}
          echo ${{ inputs.input1 }}
          echo ${{ inputs.input2 }}
          echo ${{ inputs.input3 }}
          echo ${{ secrets.secret0 }}
          echo ${{ secrets.secret1 }}
```

on: push
jobs:
  call1:

  call2:

    with:
      foo: bar
  call3:
    uses: org/repo/workflow.yml@v1
    secrets:
      foo: bar
  call4:
    uses: org/repo/workflow.yml@v1
    with:
      foo: bar
    secrets:
      foo: bar
  call5:
    name: Test

  workflow_call:

  workflow_call:
    outputs:
      uses-output1:
        description: "test"
        value: ${{ jobs.job1.outputs.output1 }}
      uses-output2:
        description: "test"
        value: ${{ jobs.job1.outputs.output2 }}
      uses-job2:
        description: "test"
        value: ${{ jobs.job2.outputs.output1 }}
jobs:
  job1:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      output1: "${{ steps.hello.outputs.foo }}"
      output2: "${{ steps.bye.outputs.foo }}"
    steps:
      - run: echo hello
        id: hello
      - run: echo bye
        id: bye
  job2:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      output1: "${{ steps.hello.outputs.foo }}"
    steps:
      - run: echo hello
        id: hello

## File: `testdata/ok/workflow_call_with_strategy.yaml`
```yaml
name: Reusable workflow with matrix strategy

# This was enabled by https://github.blog/changelog/2022-08-22-github-actions-improvements-to-reusable-workflows-2/
#
# Issue: #197
# Doc: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/reusing-workflows#example-matrix-strategy-with-a-reusable-workflow

on:
  push:

jobs:
  ReusableMatrixJobForDeployment:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        target: [dev, stage, prod]
    uses: octocat/octo-repo/.github/workflows/deployment.yml@main
    with:
      target: ${{ matrix.target }}
```
